description: Create player character and enemy sprites with basic animations. Player needs 4-directional walk cycle, attack, and hurt frames. Enemies need idle, move, attack, and death frames for 2 factions (bots and mutants).
acceptance_criteria: Player character sprite has: 4-direction idle frames, 4-direction walk cycle (4 frames each), attack animation (3 frames), and hurt flash.
Station defense bot sprites exist for: patrol drone, security bot, and turret — each with idle, move/rotate, attack, and destroyed states.
Mutated crew sprites exist for: basic mutant, armored mutant, and fast runner — each with idle, move, attack, and death animations.
All enemy sprites have at least 3 animation frames per action state.
Death animations end with a corpse/wreckage sprite that remains on the tile.
All sprites use the spec color palette and are 32x32 pixels.
Sprites are packed into a character spritesheet with a JSON mapping file.
spec_section: 7.3 Enemy Design Philosophy, A.2.4 Animation Budget, 11.1 MVP Scope (Art)
notes: Spec says '8-10 enemy types' for MVP. Start with 6 (3 per faction). Spec animation budget: 3-4 frames per action per enemy type. Total ~120-150 character sprites.
